prompt: The varargs method prints wrong results when called with an array. What is the bug?
choices: Varargs methods cannot accept int primitives; use Integer instead
Passing an int[] directly to a varargs int... parameter works correctly because Java auto-expands it
The for-each loop cannot iterate over a varargs parameter
sum(data) compiles and runs correctly; int[] is directly compatible with int... and the values are summed
explanation: In Java, an int[] passed to an int... varargs parameter is treated as the underlying array directly — the compiler does not wrap it in another array. So sum(data) works correctly and returns 6. This is a common misconception; the code is actually correct.
